philanthropy.name: Bezos Earth Fund 2030 Decisive Decade Grants

2026.methodology: 2026 Income is calculated as a net loss due to a $16B single-day drop in Feb 2026 and subsequent Q1 volatility, totaling an estimated $31B unrealized loss, offset by $4.87B in realized cash from a trading plan active through May 29, 2026. Spending follows the established $4B/year Blue Origin burn rate plus accelerated Earth Fund outlays as the fund aims for its $10B target by 2030.

philanthropy.name: Bezos Earth Fund & AI Grand Challenge

tax_and_reinvestment.name: Capital Gains Tax & Family Office Reinvestment

2025.methodology: Income calculated as the delta in Net Worth (from $174.3B to $239.4B per Forbes/Bloomberg 2025 indices) plus $10.15B in realized cash from scheduled 10b5-1 stock sales (notably June/July 2025 tranches). Spending includes a baseline $4B annual injection into Blue Origin, $1.05B in confirmed philanthropic grants, and estimated tax obligations on realized gains.

liquidity_reserve.name: Cash Reserve & Taxes on Realized Gains

2024.methodology: Income reflects the estimated annual increase in net worth (~$60B) as reported by Bloomberg/Forbes, primarily driven by Amazon stock performance. Spending is calculated based on the $13.6B in realized proceeds from stock sales, which Bezos utilizes to fund Blue Origin (avg. $3B/year), climate pledges, and private equity via Bezos Expeditions.
